model.name: DREAM S2 news-cycle retention
model.lambda_interpretation: lambda = elapsed hours since topic attention peak
model.signal_preprocessing: Formal S2 fit uses circadian-corrected publish-time article counts: raw count divided by expected global publishing/activity factor.
model.observation_clock: article.published_at, not GitHub Action run time
model.history_role: data/history.json retains raw article records and first_seen/last_seen provenance; publication time reconstructs the curve.
model.comparison: Delta AIC = AIC(exponential beta=1) - AIC(stretched S2 beta free)
model.cycle_archive_role: data/cycles.json stores completed formal cycle summaries so prior S2 learning remains visible when a new wave resets the current topic.

articles.title: Back to the noughties for young fashion-conscious England fans
articles.summary: For many watching their team beat Norway at a south London nightclub the look was as important as the game The Carpet Shop nightclub in Peckham, south London, is ordinarily packed with rowdy crowds at the weekend. But Saturday night’s liveliness wasn’t congregated around the DJ on the dancefloor, the crowd was at the sold-out venue for England’s victorious quarter-final game at the 2026 World Cup, and the young spectators were there for the fashion as much as they were for the football. articles.summary: Tehran reportedly attacks Gulf countries following fresh US strikes There has been almost no visible traffic in the strait of Hormuz so far today, with only two oil products tankers seen approaching the narrow waterway, according to a Bloomberg report . articles.title: Ukrainian drone strikes force Russia to suspend shipping in Sea of Azov
articles.summary: Vital maritime corridor closes after 90 vessels – including shadow fleet oil tankers – are attacked in under a week Russia has been forced to suspend shipping in the Sea of Azov after 90 vessels were targeted by Ukrainian drones in less than a week. Ukraine’s drone forces chief, Robert Brovdi, said on Sunday that his units had hit 10 tankers and four ferries overnight, as well as a major oil refinery in the city of Syzran.
